name: SchNetPack (Deep Learning for Molecules and Materials)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Kristof Schütt / TU Berlin

performance.notes: PyTorch GPU-accelerated; message passing O(n*k) in atoms and neighbors

agent_readiness.notes: Graph neural network-based ML interatomic potentials. import schnetpack as spk. from schnetpack.datasets import QM9. data = QM9('./qm9.db', batch_size=100). nn = spk.representation.SchNet(n_atom_basis=128). output = spk.atomistic.Atomwise(n_in=128, output_key=spk.properties.energy). model = spk.model.NeuralNetworkPotential(representation=nn, output_modules=[output]). Training via PyTorch Lightning. spk.interfaces.SpkCalculator for ASE MD. SchNet, PaiNN, FieldSchNet architectures. Forces via autograd.

gotchas: Complex training pipeline — requires PyTorch Lightning and ASE knowledge
Forces via autograd — requires careful property handling
Custom datasets require converting to ASE database format (.db)
best_when: Training SchNet/PaiNN ML interatomic potentials on molecular datasets
Custom ML force field training with equivariant GNNs
When ASE-compatible potential calculator is needed
avoid_when: Using pre-trained universal potentials (use MACE-MP-0 or CHGNet)
Without ML force field / GNN background
When training data generation pipeline is not set up
summary: SchNetPack trains graph neural network ML interatomic potentials. SchNet/PaiNN representations + Atomwise output. PyTorch Lightning training. SpkCalculator for ASE MD integration. Forces via autograd. MIT by Kristof Schütt/TU Berlin. For training custom ML potentials on QM9, MD17, or custom datasets. Complex pipeline. For pre-trained universal potentials, use MACE-MP-0 or CHGNet instead.
